segments.text: Jesus left Capernaum and went to the region of Judea and Transjordan. Once again people flocked to see him, and he was teaching them like he always did.
Some Pharisees came to see him. They tried to test him by asking the question, {
Is divorce legal?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: What did Moses tell you to do?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he asked in reply.
Moses allowed a man to write a certificate of divorce and send the woman away,
segments.speaker: [^50]they

segments.text: [^50] they replied.
Then Jesus told them, {
Moses only wrote down this rule for you because of your hard-hearted attitude.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: However, in the beginning, from creation, God made male and female.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: That's why a man leaves his father and mother and is joined in marriage to his wife,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and the two become one body. They are no longer two but one. [^51]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Let no one separate what God has joined together.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When they were back indoors, the disciples began asking him about this.
Any man who divorces his wife and marries again commits adultery against her,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he told them.
And if the wife divorces her husband and marries again she commits adultery.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Some people brought their children to Jesus so that he could bless them, but the disciples told them off and tried to keep the children away from Jesus.
But when Jesus saw what they were doing, he became very upset and told them, {
Let the children come to me! Don't stop them, for the kingdom of God belongs to those who are like these children.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: I tell you the truth, anyone who doesn't welcome the kingdom of God like a child won't enter it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: He hugged the children, placed his hands on them, and blessed them.
As Jesus set out on his journey, [^52] a man came running over and kneeled down before him. {
Good teacher, what should I do to make sure I have eternal life?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: he asked.
Why do you call me good?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus asked him.
No one is good, only God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You know the commandments: you shall not murder, you shall not commit adultery, you shall not steal, you shall not give false testimony, you shall not cheat, honor your father and mother…
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Teacher,
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: the man replied,
I've obeyed all these commandments since I was small.
segments.speaker: The man

segments.text: Jesus looked at him with love and said, {
You're only missing one thing. Go and sell everything you own, give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come and follow me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: At this, the man's face fell, and he left feeling very sad, for he was very wealthy.
Jesus looked around, and said to his disciples, {
It's only with difficulty that wealthy people enter the kingdom of God!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The disciples were shocked at this. But Jesus went on, {
My friends, it is difficult to enter God's kingdom.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: It's easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for the rich to enter God's kingdom.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The disciples were even more confused. {
Then who on earth can be saved?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked one another.
Looking right at them, Jesus replied, {
From a human point of view, it's impossible—but not with God. Everything is possible with God.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Peter spoke up, {
We've left everything to follow you…
segments.speaker: Peter

segments.text: I tell you the truth,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied,
anyone who has left behind their home or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or lands because of me, and for the sake of the good news,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: will receive in return in due course a hundred times as many homes and brothers and sisters and children and lands—as well as persecution. In the world to come they will receive eternal life.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: However, many of the first will be last, and the last first.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They continued on their way to Jerusalem, with Jesus walking on ahead. The disciples were apprehensive and the other followers were afraid. So Jesus took the disciples aside and began to explain to them what was about to happen to him.
We're going to Jerusalem,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he told them,
and the Son of man will be betrayed to the chief priests and religious teachers. They will condemn him to death and hand him over to the foreigners. [^54]
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: They will mock him, spit on him, flog him, and kill him. But three days later he will rise again.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: James and John, the sons of Zebedee, came to see him. {
Teacher,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they said,
We want you to do for us whatever we ask you.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: So what do you want me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
When you're victorious and sit on your throne, [^55] make sure we sit beside you, one on the right, the other on the left,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they told him.
You don't know what you're asking,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: replied Jesus.
Can you drink the cup I drink? Can you be baptized with the baptism of pain I will suffer?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Yes, we can,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they replied.
You will drink the cup I drink, and you will be baptized with the same baptism as me,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus told them.
But it's not for me to grant who should sit on my right or on my left. These places are reserved for those for whom they have been prepared.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When the other ten disciples heard about this, they started getting upset with James and John.
Jesus called the disciples together and told them, {
You realize that those who claim to rule over nations oppress their people. The rulers act like tyrants.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But for you it's not like this. Anyone of you who wants to be a ruler must be your servant,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: and anyone who wants to be first among you must be the slave of all of you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For even the Son of man did not come to be served but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus and his disciples passed through Jericho, and as they were leaving town, accompanied by a large crowd, Bartimaeus, [^56] a blind beggar, was sitting at the roadside.
When he heard it was Jesus of Nazareth, he started shouting out, {
Jesus, son of David, please have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Lots of people told him to be quiet, but that only made him shout even more, {
Jesus, son of David, please have mercy on me!
segments.speaker: Lots of people

segments.text: Jesus stopped, and said, {
Tell him to come here.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So they called him over, telling him,
Good news! [^57] Get up. He's calling for you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Bartimaeus jumped up, threw off his coat, and rushed over to Jesus.
What do you want me to do for you?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus asked him.
Teacher,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he said to Jesus,
I want to see!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You can go. Your trust in me has healed[^58] you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Immediately Bartimaeus could see and he followed Jesus as he went on his way.
